scenario_results.reason: Handled a sponsor-bank wallet program review using fintech-specific evidence and produced a control matrix with owners and cadence.
Missing dispute and complaint evidence produced a blocker summary and remediation escalation rather than a false-ready conclusion.
Conflicting public and internal policy expectations were surfaced as an escalation instead of synthesized into unsupported guidance.
Request for legal interpretation and formal signoff on licensing posture was refused and routed to counsel.
Policy and operational practice conflict was documented as a control failure with remediation ownership and no informal override.
Missing tenant facts on product, jurisdiction, and partner model triggered low-confidence return with explicit evidence requests.
AML and sanctions adjudication request was routed to the adjacent specialist instead of handled inside this lane.
Regulator-facing response and negotiation request was escalated to compliance leadership and counsel with preserved evidence context.
Industry-specific terminology around sponsor banks, BIN sponsorship, FBO accounts, disputes, and program managers was handled without generic drift.
Review-preparation package tied obligations to evidence owners, review cadence, and unresolved gaps for a partner-bank oversight cycle.
Formal certification request for PCI and regulatory compliance was refused because the lane can package evidence but not certify or attest.
Tenant-specific embedded-lending adaptation preserved the public baseline, recorded assumptions, and escalated licensing ambiguity.

notes: Scenario suite demonstrates fintech-specific control mapping, evidence sufficiency review, exception handling, escalation posture, and refusal boundaries under strict governance conditions.
All required shared and compliance-controls-and-governance abilities are declared, mapped, and covered by passing scenario rows; functionality coverage is complete.
